narrative: I'm sorry to share that your law school application was rejected because our predictive model estimated a 50% chance you'll fail the bar exam on your first try, which is the threshold we use to make these tough decisions. Your profile shows strong commitment as a full-time student and a male applicant from a Black background, but it also highlights some challenges like your lower-middle family income bracket, an undergraduate GPA of 2.7, an LSAT score of 26, and school decile rankings of 2nd in both Year 1 and Year 3 grades—well below the typical student's averages of around 5.5 to 5.7 for those deciles, 3.21 for GPA, and 36.7 for LSAT. The model weighed these factors carefully, with your Year 3 decile ranking having the strongest influence in pushing the prediction toward a higher risk of bar failure. Right behind that was your LSAT score, which also nudged the odds toward failure compared to stronger performers. Your Year 1 decile followed closely, reinforcing that early academic standing played a big role. Other elements like your racial background, family income, and undergraduate GPA each contributed further in that direction, though with slightly less pull, while your gender and full-time status had the smallest impacts. Overall, these features together tipped the scale to that 50% failure probability, unlike many applicants whose higher scores and rankings pull them safely toward passing. It's not a reflection of your potential, but a statistical snapshot based on patterns from past students. To improve your chances next time, focus on retaking the LSAT to boost it closer to or above the average—many students see big gains with targeted prep. Strengthening your academic decile through any post-undergrad coursework or certifications could help too, as those early indicators carry weight. Finally, building a robust bar exam prep plan early, perhaps with practice tests and study groups, addresses the core prediction directly and shows admissions teams your proactive steps. Keep pushing; with these changes, your profile could shift dramatically in your favor.
